defects: String-valued or internally contradictory audit outcomes could avoid immediate revocation because the processor used identity checks without first enforcing boolean types.
Corrected-gold references could be arbitrary truthy strings and incorrectly increase retraining correction counts.
Weekly audit and pseudo-label builders trusted lifecycle fields without a shared schema, winner-ranking, mask-hash, and stage-root containment proof.
Pseudo-label winner paths could be absolute or traverse outside the lifecycle stage root.
repairs: Require exact booleans for human_defect, serious_defect, and distribution_drift; serious defects must also be human defects.
Require corrected_gold_sha256 to be null or a lowercase 64-character SHA-256.
Reject missing or duplicate audit queue record IDs before outcome processing.
Centralize lifecycle winner verification: schema, contained relative path, current mask SHA-256, unique selected ranking row, and matching winner hash.
Apply the shared lifecycle proof before weekly audit sampling and before any calibrated pseudo-label enters the train-only manifest.

root_full.result: 757 passed after one concurrent-edit collection race was identified and rerun on a settled snapshot

clean_publish_full.command: pytest -q with clean-worktree source on PYTHONPATH

regressions: Non-boolean serious/human outcomes are rejected instead of silently evaluating false.
Fake corrected-gold hashes are rejected before retraining counts are computed.
Thirty real calibrated lifecycle sidecars with hash-verified masks produce the deterministic 20-case weekly sample.
A pseudo-label lifecycle path containing parent traversal is rejected before manifest creation.
